content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 12, 1989

Aldo Pineschi
Paul Kinney Productions
1990 3rd Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File A-89-578

Dear Mr. Pineschi:

You have requested advice on behalf of San Francisco
Supervisor Jim Gonzalez concerning the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

May Supervisor Gonzalez make contributions from his
officeholder expense committee, in the amount of $2500, to support
Proposition D, a local initiative?

CONCLUSION

Supervisor Gonzalez may use campaign funds, including
contributions received for officeholder expenses, to make
contributions to a committee formed to support Proposition D.
Contributions made in support of a ballot measure are not subject
to any limits under the Act.

FACTS

You indicated to me in our telephone conversation of October
11, 1989, that you represent Supervisor Jim Gonzalez and that you
are requesting advice in his behalf. You also stated that
Supervisor Gonzalez would like to make a contribution from his
officeholder expense committee to "Yes on Proposition D," a
committee primarily formed to support Proposition D on the City
and County of San Francisco's November ballot.

1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

ANALYSIS

Proposition 73, which was passed by the voters at the 1988
state primary election, prohibits officeholders, candidates and
their controlled committees from making contributions or loans
from campaign funds to any other officeholder, candidate or
controlled committee. (Section 85304.) However, officeholders
and candidates are not prohibited from using campaign funds to
make contributions to committees primarily formed to support or
oppose ballot measures, so long as those committees do not use the
funds to support or oppose other officeholders or candidates.

Proposition 73 also imposes a limit of $2,500 per fiscal year
on contributions made to recipient committees, including political
committees, broad based committees and political parties, which
make contributions to candidates for elective office. (Sections
85102 and 85302.) However, the Act does not impose limits on
contributions made to committees which are primarily formed to
support or oppose ballot measures and which do not make
contributions to candidates for elective office.

If you have any further questions regarding this letter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
    Political Reform Consultant

Paul Kinney Productions

Scrapero Washington, D.C.

UPO 343 N. Non 800

Scrapero LAUSL4

JPL 11 xxxx

FPP&D

Oct 5 9 ys Hn '69


October 3, 1989


Fair Political Practices
attn: Wayne
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Wayne:

First of all, thank you very much for your help in answering
my questions concerning the Supervisors of San Francisco and
Proposition D.

As per our conversation of this afternoon, I now submit a written
version of my previous question.

--Can the Supervisors of the City and County of San Francisco
contribute money (in this case $2,500 per Supervisor) from their
"Friends of" Committee-this being their officeholder expense committee to Proposition D, the pay raise for the Supervisors issue?

Once I have received written verification from you, I will pass this
information on to the Supervisors.

Again, thank you for your assistance, and please call me if you have
any questions.


Cordially,

Aldo Pineschi
Paul Kinney Productions
447-8868

Paul Kinney Productions

Sacramento · Washington, D.C.

1990 3rd St., Suite 800

Sacramento, CA 95814

916-447-8868

89-548

FPPC
Oct 5 9 42 AM '89

October 3, 1989

Fair Political Practices
attn: Wayne
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Wayne:

First of all, thank you very much for your help in answering
my questions concerning the Supervisors of San Francisco and
Proposition D.

As per our conversation of this afternoon, I now submit a written
version of my previous question.

--Can the Supervisors of the City and County of San Francisco
contribute money (in this case $2,500 per Supervisor) from their
"Friends of" Committee-this being their officeholder expense committee to Proposition D), the pay raise for the Supervisors issue?

Once I have received written verification from you, I will pass this
information on to the Supervisors.

Again, thank you for your assistance, and please call me if you have
any questions.

Cordially,

Aldo Pineschi
Paul Kinney Productions
447-8868

October 10, 1989


Aldo Pineschi
Paul Kinney Productions
1990 - 3rd Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-578

Dear Mr. Pineschi:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on October 5, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Chief Technical Assistance
                                        and Analysis Division
